name: sentry-automation
description: Automate Sentry tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): manage issues/events, configure alerts, track releases, monitor projects and teams. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: List and investigate Sentry issues across organizations and projects
Create and manage alert rules for projects and organizations
Track software releases and deployments with source maps
Monitor and update cron job monitoring configurations
Query issues using Sentry search syntax (is:unresolved, assigned:me)
List and manage organization teams and member visibility
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill provides legitimate automation for Sentry error tracking through Composio Rube MCP. The static findings flagged as external_commands are false positives - they detect MCP tool names in documentation (e.g., SENTRY_LIST_AN_ORGANIZATIONS_ISSUES), not shell execution. The network reference is to a documented MCP server endpoint (rube.app/mcp) that users explicitly configure. No actual security risks identified.
valueStatement: This skill enables Claude to manage Sentry error tracking operations including issue investigation, alert configuration, release tracking, and cron monitoring through Composio Rube MCP integration.
limitations: Does not fix code bugs directly - only manages monitoring configuration
Does not access raw application database or runtime data
Does not manage billing, user permissions, or team access settings
Requires active Rube MCP connection to Sentry for functionality

useCases.title: Production Error Investigation
useCases.description: Developer uses Claude to quickly find and investigate critical errors in production by querying Sentry issues with specific filters.

useCases.title: Alert Rule Configuration
useCases.description: DevOps engineer creates and updates alert rules for new projects or modified thresholds using natural language commands.

useCases.title: Release Monitoring
useCases.description: SRE monitors new releases and their associated errors by tracking deployment events and issue correlations.

promptTemplates.title: List Recent Errors
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the sentry-automation skill to list the 10 most recent unresolved errors in my production project. Show the issue title, frequency, and last seen timestamp.

promptTemplates.title: Create Alert Rule
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new alert rule for my backend-api project that triggers when error rate exceeds 5% over 10 minutes. Notify the on-call team.

promptTemplates.title: Track New Release
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new release version 2.1.0 for my organization, mark it as deployed to production environment, and upload source maps from the dist folder.

promptTemplates.title: Cross-Project Triage
promptTemplates.prompt: Search all projects in my organization for issues tagged with 'database' that have occurred more than 50 times in the last 24 hours. Group them by project and suggest priority.

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to verify current tool schemas before workflow execution
Verify Rube MCP connection status shows ACTIVE before running any Sentry operations
Use specific query filters (is:unresolved, assigned:me, times-seen:>10) to reduce API calls and improve response time
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode organization or project names - always use lookup tools to get current slugs
Do not skip connection status verification - operations will fail if MCP connection is not active
Do not ignore rate limit responses - implement backoff to avoid throttling

faq.question: Does this skill work with self-hosted Sentry?
faq.answer: Yes, if your self-hosted Sentry instance is connected via the Rube MCP sentry toolkit. The skill works with any Sentry API-compatible endpoint.

faq.question: What authentication is needed?
faq.answer: OAuth authentication is handled through Rube when you connect your Sentry account. The skill uses the permissions granted during the OAuth flow.

faq.question: Can I manage multiple Sentry organizations?
faq.answer: Yes, specify the organization slug in each request. Use SENTRY_GET_ORGANIZATION_DETAILS to list accessible organizations.

faq.question: Can this skill create new Sentry issues?
faq.answer: No, this skill only reads and manages existing issues. To create issues from your application, configure Sentry SDK in your code.

faq.question: What notification channels are supported for alerts?
faq.answer: Alert actions depend on your Sentry organization configuration. Common channels include Slack, email, PagerDuty, and webhooks.

faq.question: How do I fix connection not active errors?
faq.answer: Run RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit sentry. If status is not ACTIVE, follow the returned auth link to complete OAuth, then verify again.
